subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, World Bank, IDB, S&P, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, BTI) + Paraguayan official (BCP, MEF, INE) + regional/Spanish-aware press (MercoPress).
A macro-stability standout (low debt, a credible central bank, low inflation, investment grade) with weak rule of law under decades of one-party dominance.

Standout features: prudent fiscals, a credible central bank, and a young population, against pervasive corruption, climate/health weakness, and rising narco-border violence. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone; Spanish primary press secondhand.

economic.notes: A genuine macro-stability standout - low debt, a credible central bank, low inflation, and investment grade.

fiscal_state.notes: Low debt (~40% GDP), a prudent fiscal tradition, and investment grade attained in 2024. (S&P; MEF)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible inflation-targeting central bank - a strength. (BCP; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The guarani is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~3% and converging to target, with a regionally stable currency. (BCP)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A top soy/beef exporter plus ~99% hydropower and some maquila, against commodity/weather dependence and landlocked river-transport reliance. (Trade.gov; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Pervasive corruption and a one-party machine, with usable statistics.

rule_of_law.notes: A low TI standing, a one-party machine, weak courts, and US-sanctioned figures. (TI; US State/Treasury)

press_freedom.notes: Formally free but with journalists murdered in narco-border zones and ownership concentration. (RSF)

political_social.notes: Durable but one-party-dominant stability and a young population, against deep inequality.

political_stability.notes: An orderly government but with one-party dominance over decades and mild backsliding signals (an NGO law). (Freedom House; BTI)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Open, lawful residency, with record applications in 2025. (MercoPress)

social_cohesion.notes: High inequality, land concentration, and indigenous marginalisation. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: Young with a favourable dependency ratio - a genuine dividend. (World Bank; INE)

geopolitical.notes: A stable, Western-aligned partner with an open economy, against a small guerrilla remnant.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable pro-US and one of the few Taiwan-recognising states - Western-aligned. (State Dept)

conflict_involvement.notes: No war, with a small guerrilla remnant in the north and narco-transit. (InsightCrime)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open economy with free capital flows, unsanctioned. (State Dept; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Drought/flood and weak public healthcare, with rising narco-border violence.

climate.notes: Landlocked but extreme heat plus Parana drought (record-low river) plus Chaco deforestation, low readiness. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: A weak, overcrowded public system, with decent affordable private care in the capital. (State Dept)

infrastructure.notes: Improving urban infrastructure but landlocked and river-transport-dependent. (Trade.gov)

crime_safety.notes: Rising border/narco-cartel violence, with cities relatively safe. (OC Index; InsightCrime)

summary: A quiet macro-stability standout: low debt, a credible inflation-targeting central bank, low inflation, investment grade, and a young population with a favourable dependency ratio, in a durable democracy with open lawful residency. Those strengths are offset by weak rule of law - pervasive corruption under a one-party machine that has dominated for decades, with US-sanctioned figures - a soy/beef/hydropower economy exposed to commodity and weather swings, a weak public health system, drought, and rising narco-border violence. Net, a balanced outcome distribution.
